name: Albus Dumbledore
description: Current Headmaster at Hogwarts (HP1). Considered the greatest wizard of modern times. Professor Dumbledore is particularly famous for his defeat of the dark wizard Grindelwald in 1945. Known for the discovery of the twelve uses of dragon’s blood and his work on alchemy with his partner, Nicolas Flamel. Professor Dumbledore enjoys chamber music and tenpin bowling.
location: Shortcut to Quirell’s office. Redeem from Fred and George for the collection of 25 beans.

name: Merlin
description: Most famous wizard of all time. Sometimes known as the Prince of Enchanters. Advisor to the Court of King Arthur.
location: Flight Lesson, located in a hiding place behind the glass stained glass window with the Hogwarts school crest.

name: Tilly Toke
description: Awarded Order of Merlin First Class for saving lives during the Ilfracombe Incident of 1932, when a dragon swooped down on a beach full of sunbathers.
location: Alahomora Practicum. The stash behind the statue of the knight.

name: Burdock Muldoon
description: Chief of Wizard’s Council 1448 — 1450.
location: Wingardium Leviosa Practicum. Second tower.

name: Adalbert Waffling
description: Famous magical theoretician.
location: Hermione’s first letter. The room with the mirror (near the exit of the Wingardium Leviosa workshop).

name: Uric the Oddball
description: Highly eccentric wizard who is famed, among other things, for wearing a jellyfish for a hat.
location: Hermione’s second letter. Redeem from Fred and George for the collection of 25 beans.

name: Derwent Shimpling
description: Ate an entire Venomous Tentacula on a bet and survived, though is still purple.
location: Hermione’s second letter. The room behind the mirror opposite the bronze wizard statue.

name: Godric Gryffindor
description: Co-founder of Hogwarts. Gave his name to one of the four Hogwarts houses.
location: Place in front of the entrance to the Herbalist Cabinet. Redeem from Fred and George for the collection of 25 beans.

name: Cornelius Agrippa
description: Celebrated wizard imprisoned by Muggles for his writings.
location: The stash behind the Herbalist’s office.

name: Newt Scamander
description: Celebrated author of Fantastic Beasts and Where to Find Them.
location: Insendio Workshop. Tree stash.

name: Edgar Stroulger
description: Inventor of the Sneakoscope.
location: Practicum Insendio. Chest over labyrinth.

name: Bowman Wright
description: Famous for developing the Golden Snitch.
location: Hagrid’s quest. A stash behind the three Common Spikes near Hagrid’s hut.

name: Herpo the Foul
description: First known creator of the Basilisk.
location: Hagrid’s quest. Three Snails stash near Hagrid’s hut.

name: Morgan Le Fay
description: King Arthur’s half sister. Dark sorceress. Enemy of Merlin.
location: Seed hunt. The trouble spot with the cave.

name: Salazar Slytherin
description: Co-founder of Hogwarts. Gave his name to one of the four Hogwarts Houses.
location: Hike for Seeds. Hagrid gives us a card for collecting all (21) seeds, so look carefully.

name: Bertie Bott
description: Inventor of Bertie Bott’s Every Flavor Beans.
location: Journey to Quirrell’s study. At the start, Fred and George beckon us into the living room to sell us another card.

name: Cassandra Vablatsky
description: Celebrated Seer and author of ‘Unfogging the Future.’
location: Lumos Workshop. Room two. The stash behind the circular stained glass window.

name: Ignatia Wildsmith
description: The witch who invented Floo powder.
location: First corridor after the Lumos workshop, the painting opposite the Grand Staircase. Redeem the card from Fred and George for 25 beans.

name: Gifford Ollerton
description: Famous giant-slayer. Killed the giant Hengist of Upper Barnton.
location: After the Lumos challenge, go to the caster class. In doing so, go through the lobby (the same hall that contains the exit from the castle. Go down the main stairs and turn left — there is a chest with this map. After taking the map, you can already go to the castering lesson.

name: Elladora Ketteridge
description: Witch who discovered the use of Gillyweed. She nearly suffocated after eating it, recovering only when she stuck her head into a bucket of water.
location: Ingredients for Snape. The trouble spot with the lifts.

name: Hengist of Woodcroft
description: Driven away from his home by Muggle persecutors, Hengist is supposed to have settled in Scotland where he founded the village of Hogsmeade. The Three Broomsticks Inn is alleged to be Hengist’s former home.
location: Ingredients for Snape. Trouble spot with bars.

name: Helga Hufflepuff
description: Co-founder of Hogwarts. Gave her name to one of the four Hogwarts houses.
location: Filch’s level. A trouble spot with invisible platforms above the blue carpet.

name: Rowena Ravenclaw
description: Co-founder of Hogwarts. Gave her name to one of the four Hogwarts houses.
location: Back to the Tower level. At the end of the level meet Fred and George and redeem the card for 25 beans.

name: Roderick Plumpton
description: Seeker for England’s Quidditch Team. Holds British record for fastest capture of Snitch during game: three and a half seconds.
location: Chasing Snitch. Part I. Fluff. Located in a chest.

name: Harry Potter
description: The boy who lived.
location: Automatically given at the end of the game provided 24 cards and 250 beans are collected.

name: Hesper Starkey
description: Witch who studied the use of phases of the moon in potion-making.
location: Received automatically in the tutorial level with the Whomping Willow.

name: Herman Wintringham
description: Plays lute with the popular wizarding band The Weird Sisters.
location: In the Rictusempra Challenge, in the area with the first gnome and the fire crab. Knock the fire crab into the pit with Flipendo to raise the platform, walk across the raised platform to the chest. Open the chest to get this card.

name: Glenda Chittock
description: Popular presenter of the W.W.N. (Wizarding Wireless Network) programme ‘Witching Hour’.
location: In the Rictusempra challenge, in the area with two orange snails which must be knocked into pits. Along the wall there are four fire crabs behind bars. Use Rictusempra & Flipendo on all four of them, and a chest will come down containing this card.

name: Myron Wagtail
description: Lead singer of popular wizarding band the Weird Sisters.
location: In the Rictusempra Challenge, after the first save book. Climb the stairs and open the chest on the wall to get this card.

name: Gideon Crumb
description: Plays bagpipes with the popular wizarding band The Weird Sisters.
location: In the Rictusempra Challenge, in the room where two fire crabs must be knocked into small alcoves to lower the ledges. Climb the ledge on the right and go all the way around. There should be a tiny Flipendo switch on the top of the wall. Activate it and open the chest inside to get this card.

name: Heathcote Barbary
description: Plays rhythm guitar with the popular wizarding band The Weird Sisters.
location: In the Rictusempra Challenge, in a small area just after the area with two fire crabs which must be knocked into alcoves. Open the chest to your right immediately after exiting to get this card.

name: Merton Graves
description: Plays cello with the popular wizarding band The Weird Sisters.
location: Inside a chest right before the Fire Crab trap room.

name: Orsino Thruston
description: Plays drums with the popular wizarding band the Weird Sisters.
location: Inside a chest right before the Fire Crab trap room.

name: Kirley Duke
description: Plays lead guitar with the popular wizarding band The Weird Sisters.
location: After the Fire Crab trap room, cast Flipendo on a symbol on the wall right before the save game book.

name: Donaghan Tremlett
description: Bass player with the popular wizarding band The Weird Sisters.
location: In the final room of the challenge, knock 4 Fire Crabs into the pit in the centre of the room. There will be a door with a portrait of Lockhart on it that you can cast Alohomora on.

name: Bridget Wenlock
description: Famous Arithmancer. First to establish the magical properties of the number seven.
location: In the room with a beam that goes across the floor, jump down and go right. Cast Alohomora on the lightning bolt crack in the wall to find the chest.

name: Greta Catchlove
description: Author of ‘Charm Your Own Cheese’.
location: Inside a chest after the 2nd flip/flop blocker.

name: Flavius Belby
description: Only wizard ever to survive a Lethifold attack.
location: Inside a chest in a room branching off the main hallway.

name: Fulbert the Fearful
description: Famous for being so cowardly he never ventured out of his house. Died when a Defensive Charm backfired and the roof fell in.
location: Push the snail onto the symbol on the floor to open the door to the secret area.

name: Gregory the Smarmy
description: Famous originator of Gregory’s Unctuous Unction — potion to persuade the drinker that the giver is their very best friend. Alleged to have wormed his way into King Richard’s confidence and thus made his fortune.
location: There are some moving platforms that separate once you cast Skurge on them. Jump onto them, but don't separate them yet. Cast Flipendo at the switch to open the secret area. Turn around to go to the secret area.

name: Roland Kegg
description: President of English Gobstones Team.
location: Upstairs in a big room with a door that has gnomes in it above the see-saw device.

name: Glanmore Peakes
description: Famous slayer of the Sea Serpent of Cromer.
location: Inside a chest across from the 1st secret area

name: Crispin Cronk
description: Sent to Azkaban for continuing to keep Sphinxes in his back garden despite repeated warnings.
location: Use Alohomora on the wall that looks like a face to open a door with a chest

name: Cornelius Agrippa
description: Celebrated wizard imprisoned by non-magical folks for his writings.
location: Inside a chest with 2 orange snails after the Skurge stairs puzzle

name: Honoria Nutcombe
description: Founded the Society for the Reformation of Hags.
location: Use Alohomora on another face wall to open a door

name: Miranda Goshawk
description: Famous spellbook writer.
location: Inside a chest in the storage room after the flipping doors puzzle

name: Grogan Stump
description: Popular Minister for Magic, appointed 1811.
location: Inside a chest in an alcove on the right just after the swinging cog platforms

name: Leopoldina Smethwyck
description: First British witch to referee a Quidditch match.
location: Cast Flipendo on the 4 switches to reveal a secret area

name: Merwyn the Malicious
description: Credited with the invention of many unpleasant jinxes and hexes.
location: Inside a chest in the room with pixies and an orange snail

name: Uric the Oddball
description: Highly eccentric wizard who is famed, among other things, for wearing a jellyfish for a hat.
location: Cast Diffindo on a rope to drop a beam. Walk along the beam into the corridor. Take a right and go forward. It's in the chest.

name: Derwent Shimpling
description: Ate an entire Venomous Tentacula for a bet and survived, though is still purple.
location: From that room, climb the stairs. Push the block so it falls. Go back down the stairs and push the block to reveal a secret area.

name: Blenheim Stalk
description: Non-magical expert and author of many books including ‘Non-Magical Who Notice,’ a study of non-magical awareness of magic.
location: In the courtyard where Bowtruckles are introduced, there is a carved image on the wall. Cast Diffindo on the rope closer to you so you can climb it. Climb up the ramp and take the path left. Cast Alohomora to reveal the secret area and find the chest.

name: Elladora Ketteridge
description: Witch who discovered the use of Gillyweed when she nearly suffocated after eating it and recovered only when she stuck her head into a bucket of water.
location: There is a bridge being held by 2 ropes. Cross the bridge and cast Alohomora on the flower wall in front of you. Climb down the ledges and open the chest.

name: Lord Stoddard Withers
description: Breeder of flying horses.
location: After the courtyard with the Venemous Tentaculas, go through the doorway and down the hall to the flower wall. Cast Alohomora and open the chest.

name: Thaddeus Thurkell
description: Famous for producing seven Squib sons and turning them all into hedgehogs in disgust.
location: In the trap corridor with 3 orange snails, cast Rictusempra and Flipendo on 2 of them. Put them on the snail plate/trap and then turn around and pass the snail. Cast Alohomora on the lightning bolt crack in the wall. Flip this snail onto its plate and open the chest.

name: Sacharissa Tugwood
description: Pioneer of Beautifying Potions. Discovered the pimple-curing properties of Bubotuber Pus.
location: Cut the ropes so the platform will move. Hit the switch to reveal a Lumos gargoyle. Cast at it, run across the platform, and turn right to find the secret area with the card.

name: Tilly Toke
description: Gained Order of Merlin, First Class for saving non-magical lives during the Ilfracombe Incident of 1932, when a dragon swooped down on a beach full of sunbathers.
location: Inside a chest in the greenhouse.

name: Roderick Plumpton
description: Seeker for England Quidditch Team. Holds British record for fastest capture of Snitch during game: three and a half seconds.
location: Inside a chest in the yard with Bowtruckles at the beginning of the quest

name: Ignatia Wildsmith
description: Witch who invented Floo powder.
location: In the study, climb up the chimney. Cast Flipendo on the loose block. Push it towards the bookcase. Go into the alcove and cast Lumos on the gargoyle. Climb the block and the bookcase. Jump into the alcove to open the chest.

name: Beatrix Bloxam
description: Author of the Toadstool Tales, a series of childrens’ books since banned because they have been found to cause nausea and vomiting.
location: In the area with the ramp and the orange snails, go through the door and defeat the Fire Crabs and Snails to make the platforms rise.

name: Devlin Whitehorn
description: Founder of the Nimbus racing broom company.
location: Just past the library, turn right and cast Alohomora on the lock in the niche above the wall. Walk up the steps and go to the gargoyle. Go behind it to open the chest.

name: Edgar Stroulger
description: Inventor of the Sneakoscope.
location: Use Flipendo on the wooden planks so the red side faces you. Go into the left alcove once it's open.

name: Circe
description: Lived on Island of Aeaea. Expert at turning lost sailors into pigs.
location: Once outside from the stable rafters, jump up to the platform on the left then follow the ledge around to the right. Cast Diffindo on the rope. Drop down below and go through the open door. Cast Diffindo again on the rope above the wooden platform to drop the chest.

name: Elfrida Clagg
description: Chieftaness of Warlock’s Council.
location: When the bridge falls, turn right and cast Flipendo on the wall. Cast Flipendo again. Turn right and go down the stairs. Cast Flipendo on the 3 carvings and go back up the stairs. Jump across the ledges on the right to get to the platform with the chest.

name: Adalbert Waffling
description: Famous magical theoretician.
location: Use Lumos on the gargoyle and go left. Jump on the floating platforms to the left of the railing. Inside the secret area is the chest.

name: Justus Pilliwickle
description: Celebrated Head of the Department of Magical Law Enforcement.
location: In the library, hit the switch that looks like a lock to lower the bookshelves so you can access the chest.

name: Morgan le Fay
description: King Arthur’s half sister. Dark sorceress. Enemy of Merlin.
location: Cast at the 2 Slytherin emblems. The dragon statue in between will reveal the secret area where the card is contained.

name: Yardley Platt
description: Serial goblin-killer.
location: In the room with the waterfall, cast Alohomora on the face to open a secret area where the chest is contained.

name: Merlin
description: Most famous wizard of all time. Sometimes known as the Prince of Enchanters. Part of the Court of King Arthur.
location: In the 1st room with the gnomes, after throwing them into their holes and casting Lumos on the gargoyle, jump onto the ledge. It's in the chest.

name: Xavier Rastrick
description: Flamboyant wizard entertainer who vanished unexpectedly while tapdancing to a crowd of three hundred in Painswick and was never seen again.
location: After getting the previous card, in that room, turn right and cast Alohomora on the odd brick. Climb the ledge and jump across into the wall opening. It's in the chest.

name: Quong Po
description: Chinese wizard who discovered the uses of powdered Fireball eggs.
location: Defeat the 3 imps to reveal a gargoyle. Cast Lumos to find the secret area.

name: Dymphna Furmage
description: Famously abducted by pixies while on holiday in Cornwall and lived in mortal dread of them thereafter. Failed to persuade the Ministry of Magic to have all pixies humanely destroyed.
location: Defeat the 4 Fire Crabs to create a pathway.

name: Felix Summerbee
description: Inventor of Cheering Charms.
location: In the room with all the pictures, cast Flipendo on an upside down one. Open the chest.

name: Alberic Grunnion
description: Inventor of the Dungbomb.
location: Use Lumos to reveal the secret stairs. Cast Flipendo on the switch to make the 2 doors open. Use the Spongify tile to get to the other room.

name: Celestina Warbeck
description: Popular singing sorceress.
location: After the Spider Trap room with the 2 spiders and 2 Flipendo switches and after Lumos, in the large room where the Flipendo switch is, after getting the Beans behind it, turn left and cast Alohomora on the chest in the alcove.

name: Hengist of Woodcroft
description: Driven away from his home by non-magical persecutors, Hengist is supposed to have settled in Scotland where he founded the village of Hogsmeade. The Three Broomsticks inn is alleged to be Hengist’s old home.
location: In the final room of the challenge, destroy the spider web above you and use Flipendo on the switch to reveal a Spongify tile. Jump on it to get to the secret room on the left.

name: Cliodne
description: Irish druidess who discovered properties of moondew.
location: At the beginning of the level, cast Lumos on the gargoyle to reveal a Spongify tile. Cast Spongify on it and bounce to the left log. Cast Diffindo on the web and use Spongify again. Do the same on the other side. Spongify the next tile to bounce down to the logs. Jump back to the 1st log (towards the gargoyle) and look to the right where there's a symbol. Cast Flipendo and then open the chest in the secret area.

name: Gifford Ollerton
description: Famous giant-slayer. Killed the giant Hengist of Upper Barnton.
location: When you get to the section with the tree, keep bouncing on the Spongify tiles until you go inside the tree. Once inside the tree, Spongify up to the high branch.

name: Gondoline Oliphant
description: Famous for studies of life and habits of trolls. Clubbed to death in the Cotswolds while sketching.
location: Kill the 3 Spiders to access the secret area. It's in the chest.

name: Perpetua Fancourt
description: Witch who invented the Lunascope.
location: Inside a chest in the cave with many webs and spiders.

name: Dorcas Wellbeloved
description: Founder of the Society for Distressed Witches.
location: In the chest above the Bowtruckle clearing.

name: Newt Scamander
description: Celebrated author of ‘Fantastic Beasts and Where to Find Them’.
location: After dropping down the pit on the log, turn around and face the other direction. Cast Diffindo and then open the chest.

name: Wilfred Elphick
description: First wizard to be gored by an African Erumpent.
location: On 1st beam in orange snails trapdoor room.

name: Bertie Bott
description: Inventor of Bertie Bott’s Every Flavour Beans.
location: Found by completing the challenge on the first floor once all 40 of the Silver Wizard Cards have been found.

name: Albus Dumbledore
description: Headmaster of Hogwarts.
location: Found by completing the challenge on the first floor once all 40 of the Silver Wizard Cards have been found.

name: Godric Gryffindor
description: Co-founder of Hogwarts. Gave his name to one of the four Hogwarts houses.
location: Found by completing the challenge on the first floor once all 40 of the Silver Wizard Cards have been found.

name: Herpo the Foul
description: First known creator of the basilisk.
location: Found by completing the challenge on the first floor once all 40 of the Silver Wizard Cards have been found.

name: Salazar Slytherin
description: Co-founder of Hogwarts. Gave his name to one of the four Hogwarts Houses.
location: Found by completing the challenge on the first floor once all 40 of the Silver Wizard Cards have been found.

name: Helga Hufflepuff
description: Co-founder of Hogwarts. Gave her name to one of the four Hogwarts houses.
location: Found by completing the challenge on the first floor once all 40 of the Silver Wizard Cards have been found.

name: Montague Knightley
description: Wizard Chess Champion.
location: Found by completing the challenge on the first floor once all 40 of the Silver Wizard Cards have been found.

name: Paracelsus
description: Contemporary of Copernicus and Leonardo Da Vinci, a medical genius whose bold theories Challenged medieval thought. Credited with discovering Parseltongue.
location: Found by completing the challenge on the first floor once all 40 of the Silver Wizard Cards have been found.

name: Carlotta Pinkstone
description: Famous campaigner for lifting the International Confederation of Wizard’s Statute of Secrecy and telling non-magical folks that wizards still exist. Ms. Pinkstone has been imprisoned several times for her blatant and deliberate use of magic in public places.
location: Found by completing the challenge on the first floor once all 40 of the Silver Wizard Cards have been found.

name: Rowena Ravenclaw
description: Co-founder of Hogwarts. Gave her name to one of the four Hogwarts houses.
location: Found by completing the challenge on the first floor once all 40 of the Silver Wizard Cards have been found.

name: Harry Potter
description: The boy who lived.
location: Found by completing the challenge on the first floor once all 40 of the Silver Wizard Cards have been found.

name: Norvel Twonk
description: Died saving a non-magical child from a runaway Manticore. Posthumously awarded the Order of Merlin, First Class.
location: Near the entrance to the Diffindo challenge, use Alohomora and Diffindo to access the secret area.

name: Mirabella Plunkett
description: Famous for falling in love with a merman in Loch Lomond while on holiday. When her parents forbade her to marry him, she transfigured herself into a haddock and was never seen again.
location: Cast Lumos on the gargoyle where the previous card was and then run towards the Hogwarts main entrance. Take a right and inside the wall, the gargoyle revealed some steps. Go up the steps and open the chest.

name: Cyprian Youdle
description: Only Quidditch referee ever to die during a match. The originator of the curse was never caught but believed to have been a member of the crowd.
location: Take the path towards the greenhouse. Next to the trader, there's a switch high up on the wall. Hit it to open a secret area.

name: Gulliver Pokeby
description: Expert on magical birds. First to identify meaning of Augurey song.
location: Inside a chest to the left of the greenhouse.

name: Beaumont Marjoribanks
description: Pioneer of Herbology and collector of many rare and magical flowers. Discovered Gillyweed.
location: Go through the Herbology greenhouse and follow the path to the right. Cast Diffindo on the vines and open the chest.

name: Joscelind Wadcock
description: Chaser for Puddlemere United Quidditch Team. Record for highest number of goals during British season this century (against Ballycastle Bats, 1931).
location: From the large dragon statue, turn right and head towards the Quidditch Pitch gates. Cast Alohomora on the 2 banners hanging from each side of the gate. This opens a secret area in the left wall. It's in the chest.

name: Jocunda Sykes
description: Famous for flying across the Atlantic on a broomstick -the first person to do so.
location: Left of the Hogwarts main entrance, a Bronze Wizard Card on a ledge is visible. Use the Spongify tile to leap up and get the card.

name: Dunbar Oglethorpe
description: Chief of Q.U.A.B.B.L.E. (Quidditch Union for the Administration and Betterment of the British League and its Endeavours).
location: In the area where you found the Mirabella Plunkett card, there is a grey square tile. Jump on it to open a secret area, then run down the stairs and use the Spongify tile to reach the card.

name: Havelock Sweeting
description: Unicorn expert. Helped set up unicorn reservations throughout Britain.
location: At Hagrid's Hut, jump onto the tall tree stump.

name: Gunhilda of Gorsemoor
description: One-eyed, hump-backed witch famous for developing a cure for Dragon pox.
location: Inside a chest in the Potions Classroom.

name: Laverne de Montmorency
description: Inventor of many Love Potions.
location: In the Potions Classroom, cast Alohomora on the wall to reveal a door. Cast Skurge to reveal a switch. Step on the switch and a staircase will appear, leading to a secret area.

name: Gaspard Shingleton
description: Celebrated inventor of the Self-Stirring Cauldron.
location: In the dungeon corridor, cast Lumos on a gargoyle to reveal a hole in the floor near the gate at the end of the corridor. Cast Skurge on the gate so it will fall and you can reach the chest.

name: Wendelin the Weird
description: Alleged to have enjoyed being burnt at the stake so much that she allowed herself to be captured fourteen times in various disguises (the book indicated that 47 times or not 😉 ).
location: From the dungeon corridor, take a right down the hall to the room where the entrance to the Slytherin Common Room is. Cast Skurge on the ectoplasm and then cast Lumos on the gargoyle. Run over to the opposite corner and cast Diffindo on the rope. Open the chest at the bottom of the gap you just created.

name: Bowman Wright
description: Famous for developing the Golden Snitch.
location: On the floor at the top of the entrance hall stairs, cast Diffindo on a mirror to open a secret area.

name: Mopsus
description: Soothsayer who vanquished the Seer Calchas in a contest of their powers.
location: From the top of the entrance hall stairs, go to the House Point Ceremony Chamber. In the doorway leading into the room, cast Alohomora on the left wall. Cast Skurge on the ectoplasm and Diffindo on the spiderweb. Drop down to find the chest.

name: Archibald Alderton
description: Famous for blowing up the hamlet of Little Dropping in Hampshire whilst attempting to magically mix a birthday cake.
location: At the top of the entrance hall stairs, cast Spongify on the tile to jump up to the ledge with the chest.

name: Almerick Sawbridge
description: Famous for conquering the river troll that was terrorising those trying to cross the Wye River. The river troll in question was believed to be one of the largest ever to exist in Britain; having a weight of one tonne.
location: Inside a chest in the Gryffindor Common Room

name: Gwenog Jones
description: Captain and Beater of only all-female national Quidditch Team, the Holyhead Harpies.
location: In the Gryffindor Common Room, go to the top of the stairs. Cast Flipendo on the ceiling tile. In the wall in the middle of the staircase, a rope will appear that you can sever with Diffindo. After severing the rope, walk underneath the stairs and open the chest.

name: Andros the Invincible
description: Alleged to have been the only known wizard to produce a Patronus the size of a giant.
location: Outside the exit of the Skurge challenge, use Flipendo on the statue to reveal a pressure plate on the floor. Step on it to open a passage. Open the chest in this passage to get this card.

name: Daisy Dodderidge
description: First landlady of the Leaky Cauldron.
location: In Hogwarts Castle, in the area between the exits of the Skurge and Spongify challenges. Use Alohomora on an indent high up on the wall. Use Skurge to get rid of the ectoplasm on the ledges. Climb them and open the chest to get this card.

name: Ethelred the Ever-Ready
description: Famous for taking offence at nothing and cursing innocent bystanders. Died in Gaol.
location: In a chest under the stairs on the ground floor of the Grand Staircase.

name: Alberta Toothill
description: Winner of the All-England Wizarding Duelling Competition of 1430. Famously overcame the favourite, Samson Wiblin, with a Blasting Curse.
location: In the Grand Staircase main room, use the middle Spongify tile on the ground floor. The card is at the ceiling.

name: Balfour Blane
description: Established the Committee on Experimental Charms.
location: Inside a chest in the Charms Classroom.

name: Cassandra Vablatsky
description: Celebrated Seer and author of ‘Unfogging the Future’.
location: Inside a chest in Moaning Myrtle's Bathroom.

name: Burdock Muldoon
description: Chief of Wizard’s Council 1448-50.
location: Inside a chest in an alcove behind Dumbledore's desk

name: Oswald Beamish
description: Pioneer of Goblin Rights.
location: Go upstairs and cast Flipendo on the symbol under the window. Take the elevator up to the catwalk. Follow it around to get the card.

name: Musidora Barkwith
description: Composer of the unfinished Wizarding Suite, which features an exploding tuba. Its performance has been banned since its last performance in 1902, when it blew the roof out of Ackerly Town Hall.
location: There is a chest in the Defence Against the Dark Arts Classroom.

name: Artemisia Lufkin
description: First witch to become Minister for Magic.
location: On the 4th Floor, cast Alohomora to open a secret area. Cast Skurge on the ectoplasm and then sever the rope using Diffindo. Use the Spongify tile to leap to the other secret area.

name: Chauncey Oldridge
description: First known victim of Dragon Pox.
location: In Madam Pomfrey's office, use the Spongify tile to leap onto the ledge in the ceiling. It's in the chest.

name: Queen Maeve
description: Legendary witch who trained young sorcerers in Ireland prior to the establishment of Hogwarts School of Witchcraft and Wizardry.
location: In the Hospital Wing, cast Flipendo on the 4 portraits. The floor will turn into steps, leading to a secret area.

name: Glover Hipworth
description: Inventor of the Pepperup Potion, cure for the common cold.
location: In the Hospital Wing, cast Lumos on the gargoyle. Climb on the hidden block to get to the ledge where the chest is.

name: Mungo Bonham
description: Famous wizard healer. Founded Saint Mungo’s Hospital for Magical Ailments and Injuries.
location: In Madam Pomfrey's Office, there is a chest.

name: Fulbert the Fearful
description: Famous for being so cowardly he never ventured out of his house. Died when a Defensive Charm backfired and the roof fell in.
location: Hogwarts Express

name: Ethelred the Ever-Ready
description: Famous for taking offence at nothing and cursing innocent bystanders. Died in Gaol.
location: Purchase from students. Price: 80 Pumpkin Pasties

name: Crispin Cronk
description: Sent to Azkaban for continuing to keep Sphinxes in his back garden despite repeated warnings.
location: Purchase from students. Price: 100 beans

name: Edgar Stroulger
description: Inventor of the Sneakoscope.
location: Purchase from students. Price: 150 beans

name: Oswald Beamish
description: Pioneer of Goblin Rights.
location: Purchase from students. Price: 225 beans

name: Norvel Twonk
description: Died saving a Muggle child from a runaway Manticore. Posthumously awarded the Order of Merlin, First Class.
location: Purchase from students. Price: 125 beans

name: Grogan Stump
description: Popular Minister for Magic, appointed 1811.
location: Fred and George’s shop. Price: 25 Pumpkin Pasties

name: Glanmore Peakes
description: Famous slayer of the Sea Serpent of Cromer.
location: Purchase from students. Price: 60 Pumpkin Pasties

name: Wilfred Elphick
description: First wizard to be gored by an African Erumpent.
location: Purchase from students. Price: 40 Pumpkin Pasties

name: Professor Armando Dippet
description: Former headmaster of Hogwarts c.1940.
location: Search. Rescue Buckbeak. Cauldron next to the rabbit statue.

name: Mungo Bonham
description: Famous wizard healer. Founded St Mungo’s Hospital for Magical Maladies and Injuries.
location: Purchase from students. Price: 50 beans

name: Dzou Yen
description: Chinese alchemist.
location: Purchase from students. Price: 20 Pumpkin Pasties

name: Falco Aesalon
description: The first recorded example of an Animagus, he could transform himself into a falcon.
location: Search. Enter the library for the Book (protection for the hippogriff)

name: Thaddeus Thurkell
description: Famous for producing seven Squib sons and turning them all into hedgehogs in disgust.
location: Purchase from students. Price: 70 Pumpkin Pasties

name: Harry Potter
description: The boy who lived.
location: . Fred and George’s shop

name: Godric Gryffindor
description: Co-founder of Hogwarts. Gave his name to one of the four Hogwarts houses.
location: Bonus room.

name: Rowena Ravenclaw
description: Co-founder of Hogwarts. Gave her name to one of the four Hogwarts houses.
location: Bonus room.

name: Helga Hufflepuff
description: Co-founder of Hogwarts. Gave her name to one of the four Hogwarts houses.
location: Bonus room.

name: Salazar Slytherin
description: Co-founder of Hogwarts. Gave his name to one of the four Hogwarts houses.
location: Bonus room.

name: Albus Dumbledore
description: Headmaster of Hogwarts.
location: Bonus room.

name: Beatrix Bloxam
description: Author of the Toadstool Tales, a series of children’s books since banned because they have been found to cause nausea and vomiting.
location: Purchase from students. Price: 25 beans

name: Dymphna Furmage
description: Famously abducted by pixies while on holiday in Cornwall and lived in mortal dread of them thereafter. Failed to persuade the Ministry of Magic to have all pixies humanely destroyed.
location: Purchase from students. Price: 50 beans

name: Honoria Nutcombe
description: Founded the Society for the Reformation of Hags.
location: Anti-Dementor Lessons

name: Wendelin the Weird
description: Alleged to have enjoyed being burnt at the stake so much that she allowed herself to be captured forty-seven times in various disguises.
location: Purchase from students. Price: 75 beans

name: Jocunda Sykes
description: Famous for flying across the Atlantic on a broomstick -the first person to do so.
location: Purchase from students. Price: 200 beans

name: Carlotta Pinkstone
description: Famous campaigner for lifting the International Confederation of Wizard’s Statute of Secrecy and telling Muggles that wizards still exist. Ms. Pinkstone has been imprisoned several times for her blatant and deliberate use of magic in public places.
location: Purchase from students. Price: 100 beans

name: Dorcas Wellbeloved
description: Founder of the Society for Distressed Witches.
location: Purchase from students. Price: 175 beans

name: Elfrida Clagg
description: Chieftainess of Warlock’s Council.
location: Purchase from students. Price: 125 beans

name: Sacharissa Tugwood
description: Pioneer of Beautifying Potions. Discovered the pimple-curing properties of Bubotuber Pus.
location: Entering the Shrieking Shack

name: Ignatia Wildsmith
description: Witch who invented Floo powder.
location: Purchase from students. Price: 10 Pumpkin Pasties

name: Hesper Starkey
description: Witch who studied the use of phases of the moon in potion-making.
location: Fred and George’s shop. Price: 125 beans

name: Cliodne
description: Irish druidess who discovered properties of Moondew.
location: Purchase from students. Price: 30 Pumpkin Pasties

name: Mirabella Plunkett
description: Famous for falling in love with a merman in Loch Lomond while on holiday. When her parents forbade her to marry him, she transfigured herself into a haddock and was never seen again.
location: Fred and George’s shop. Price: 25 Pumpkin Pasties

name: Morgan le Fay
description: King Arthur’s half sister. Dark sorceress. Enemy of Merlin.
location: Purchase from students. Price: 50 Pumpkin Pasties

name: Gunhilda of Gorsemoor
description: One-eyed, hump-backed witch famous for developing a cure for Dragon pox.
location: Fred and George’s shop. Price: 100 beans

name: Babayaga
description: Russian hag who dwelled in a hut that stood on giant chicken legs. Ate children for breakfast — and presumably for lunch and tea.
location: Library

name: Malodora Grymm
description: Using a beautification potion to conceal her true hag form, she married a king and used a charmed mirror to reinforce her self-image. Became jealous of the most beautiful girl in the land and fed her a poisoned apple.
location: Library

name: Leticia Somnolens
description: This spiteful hag was jealous of the king’s daughter and caused her to prick her finger on a spindle tainted with a Draught of the Living Death. A young wizard who had smeared his lips with Wiggenweld potion kissed the princess and brought her out of her trance.
location: Library

name: Old Mother Hubbard
description: Lured stray animals into her home and starved them to death.
location: Library

name: Cordelia Misericordia
description: Hag representative at 14th century summit of Wizards’ Council.
location: Library

name: Imp
description: Found only in Britain and Ireland, the imp has a slapstick sense of humour and will amuse itself by pushing and tripping the unwary.
location: Search: Behind the portrait on the 7th floor

name: Doxy
description: Sometimes mistaken for a fairy. Nests in bushes and, if approached, will attempt to bite with its double rows of sharp, venomous teeth.
location: Search: Behind the portrait in the Dungeon

name: Bowtruckle
description: A tree-guardian adept at dodging most kinds of charms. May attempt to gouge out your eyes if you approach its tree home in a threatening manner.
location: Search: Behind the portrait in the Dungeon

name: Billywig
description: Has a long thin sting that causes giddiness followed by levitation. Dried Billywig stings are used in a variety of potions.
location: Search: Behind the portrait on the 1st floor

name: Gnome
description: A common garden pest, the gnome lurks in holes in the ground and may run out and attempt to trip up the unwary traveller. Can be ejected from the garden by swinging it in circles and flinging it over the garden wall.
location: Search: Behind the portrait on the 2nd floor

name: Giant Squid
description: The bane of ancient mariners and students at Hogwarts — should the latter decide to go for a dip in the lake.
location: Search: Behind the portrait on the 4th floor

name: Manticore
description: A rare, dangerous beast, the Manticore has been known to sing softly as it eats its prey. Its sting will kill instantaneously.
location: Search: Behind the portrait on the 5th floor

name: Unicorn
description: The unicorn is a shy creature and generally avoids human contact. It is more likely to let a witch come near it than a wizard and casting magic spells may frighten it off. Its blood has highly magical properties.
location: Search: Behind the portrait on the 6th floor

name: Mountain Troll
description: Frequents mountainous regions. Very powerful, but stupid, the troll will attempt to bludgeon anyone who comes near it.
location: Fred and George’s shop. Price: 150 beans

name: Streeler
description: A large, orange snail with a poisonous shell that should only be handled when wearing protective gloves.
location: Fred and George’s shop. Price: 200 beans

name: Giant Purple Toad
description: Likes dead flies and other insects. Has a long, green tongue.
location: Search: Hogwarts courtyard, alcove at the top around the corner to the right of the pendulum

name: Double-ended Newt
description: Can be purchased from the Magical Menagerie in Diagon Alley. Swims around a lot and likes algae.
location: Search: Hogwarts courtyard, in the attic

name: Gytrash
description: Large dog-like spirit that can be encountered in the Forbidden Forest, either singly or in groups.
location: Search: Hogwarts area, behind Hagrid’s hut

name: Kelpie
description: Lurks in rivers and streams and will kneel in invitation for anyone to ride it across. If this is attempted, the Kelpie will drag the rider underwater and attempt to eat them.
location: Search: Hogwarts courtyard, behind the crest, jump from the center of the courtyard

name: Phoenix
description: A gentle creature, the phoenix lives to an immense age because it can regenerate each time it bursts into flames. Phoenix song is magical and its tears have healing properties.
location: Search: Hogwarts Courtyard, Outer Courtyard Quest

name: Bran the Bloodthirsty
description: Lived in a castle on top of an enchanted Beanstalk. Apparently fond of eating bread made with ground bones and washed down with the blood of an Englishman. Was slain by a boy named Jack.
location: Hogwarts area, near the well

name: Cyclops
description: A one-eyed giant who lived in a cave at the foot of Mount Etna. With the help of several sheep, was slain by the Greek hero, Odysseus.
location: Hogwarts area, near the well

name: Goliath
description: Mercenary giant used by the Philistines in their war with the Israelites. Was slain by a young boy with a slingshot.
location: Hogwarts area, near the well

name: Morholt
description: Gigantic brother of the King of Ireland. Wounded Tristan, the Celtic hero, with a poisoned sword when the latter tried to kill him.
location: Hogwarts area, near the well

name: Hengist of Upper Barnton
description: Giant killed by the famous giant-slayer Gifford Ollerton.
location: Hogwarts area, near the well

name: Sir Herbert Varney
description: Short-lived (by undead standards) Victorian vampire who preyed upon women in London during the 1880s. Was subsequently captured and killed by a special squad from the Department for the Regulation and Control of Magical Creatures.
location: Search: Lupin’s First Lesson. 1st Hall with Platforms over the Abyss

name: Amarillo Lestoat
description: Flamboyant American vampire. Author of ‘A Vampire’s Monologue’ — intended to bore the reader into a stupor making him/her easier prey for vampires.
location: Search: Lupin’s First Lesson. 2nd hall with platforms over the abyss, niche behind

name: Lady Carmilla Sanguina
description: Bathed in the blood of her victims to retain her youthful beauty.
location: Search: Lupin’s First Lesson. 3rd Hall with Platforms Over the Abyss, Knight’s Armor

name: Blodwyn Bludd
description: Known as the ‘Vampire from the Valleys’. Famous for singing to his victims in a sonorous bass baritone, before biting their necks.
location: Search: Lupin’s First Lesson. Knight’s Armor

name: Count Vlad Drakul
description: Notorious vampire who inspired the fictional Count Dracula created by Bram Stoker. Father of Vlad the Impaler.
location: Search: Lupin’s First Lesson. Hallway, Knight’s Armor, Niche Above

name: Common Welsh Green Dragon
description: This breed is among the least troublesome of dragons and actively avoids humans unless provoked. Fire is issued in thin jets.
location: Search: McGonagall’s Lesson: Knight’s Armor

name: Hebridean Black Dragon
description: Britain’s other native dragon is more aggressive than its Welsh counterpart and feeds mostly on deer, though it has been known to carry off large dogs.
location: Search: McGonagall’s Lesson. 2nd pavilion with a rotating path, jump down, in a pumpkin

name: Hungarian Horntail Dragon
description: Supposedly the most dangerous of all dragon breeds, the Hungarian Horntail can breathe fire up to fifty feet
location: Search: McGonagall’s Lesson. The Quest with Opening the Bars

name: Romanian Longhorn Dragon
description: The horns of this breed of dragon are highly valued as potion ingredients and as a result, its numbers have fallen in recent years.
location: Search: McGonagall’s Lesson. Quest in the niche above

name: Norwegian Ridgeback Dragon
description: The Ridgeback is nowadays one of the rarer dragon breeds and has been known to attack most kinds of large land mammal.
location: Search: McGonagall’s Lesson. The Labyrinth, Inside the Central Tower

name: Eargit the Ugly
description: Goblin representative at 14th century summit of Wizards’ Council.
location: Search: Glacius Challenge. Hiding place behind the poster on the wall

name: Alguff the Awful
description: Foul-smelling goblin nuisance. Well known throughout the goblin world for trying to sell vials of his sweat to a Dungbomb manufacturer.
location: Search: Glacius Challenge. Cache in 1 room with 2 salamanders

name: Ug the Unreliable
description: Notorious goblin confidence trickster. Organised the infamous Demiguise Derby and absconded with the takings.
location: Search: Glacius Challenge. Hiding place under the gazebo with doxies.

name: Urg the Unclean
description: Rebel leader in the 18th century goblin rebellions.
location: Search: Glacius Challenge. Stairs changing direction, entrance to the right

name: Gringott
description: Founder of Gringotts wizard bank.
location: Search: Glacius Challenge. A Turn on the Last Ice Descent

name: Joscelind Wadcock
description: Chaser for Puddlemere United Quidditch Team. Record for highest number of goals during British season this century (against Ballycastle Bats, 1931).
location: Hogwarts surroundings. Hippogriff Mini-Game.

name: Gwenog Jones
description: Captain and Beater of only all-female national Quidditch Team, the Holyhead Harpies.
location: Hogwarts surroundings. Hippogriff Mini-Game.

name: Cyprian Youdle
description: Only Quidditch referee ever to be killed during a match. The originator of the curse was never caught but believed to have been a member of the crowd.
location: Hogwarts surroundings. Hippogriff Mini-Game.

name: Roderick Plumpton
description: Seeker for England Quidditch Team. Holds British record for fastest capture of Snitch during game: three and a half seconds.
location: Hogwarts surroundings. Hippogriff Mini-Game.

name: Bowman Wright
description: Famous for developing the Golden Snitch.
location: Hogwarts surroundings. Hippogriff Mini-Game.
